seed_problem: Build a connected relay network where no node has degree > 3, minimizing total cable length; checker validates connectivity+degree and scores baseline/cost.
mutation: output: keep the min-cost connected-network goal but replace the single global degree bound with heterogeneous per-node capacities c_i in {2,3,4} on geometric stations, so the optimal degree-constrained spanning network is NP-hard and greedy/local-search strategies diverge.
title: Skybridge Swarm: Degree-Bounded Relay Mesh
strategies: input-order chain baseline: link stations 1-2-3-...-n in the given order (always feasible, degree <= 2)
nearest-neighbour chain: build a Hamiltonian path by always hopping to the closest unvisited station (degree <= 2, geometry-aware)
degree-constrained Kruskal: add globally cheapest edges that join two components without exceeding any capacity, then repair leftover components using spare-capacity nodes
local-search rewiring: start from a degree-constrained spanning tree and repeatedly swap an expensive edge for a cheaper capacity-feasible reconnection
